On the television, fabulous graphics and MUSIC plays introducing "World Class Wrestling".

TV ANNOUNCER (У.0.) (FROM TV) Live from the Springfield Super Dome and Flea Market, to decide who will be Intercontinental Federal Mountain Time Zone Champion, the Wrestling Match of the Century. Today Rasputin, The Friendly Russian...

A BEARDED WRESTLER wearing a monk's robe appears on the TV screen. The AUDIENCE CHEERS.

MILHOUSE (У.0.) Hey, didn't he used to be the Mad

Russian?

BART is sitting in his living room with LEWIS, MILHOUSE and RICHARD. They are all sipping soda, munching crunchy snack food and watching wrestling on television. LISA sits ina chair off to the side, reading a math book.

On the television, Rasputin begins waving Soviet and American flags.

KRUMNOW is a huge, mad scientist-type wearing a mortarboard апа lab coat, which he tears off and discards as he enters. The kids all CHEER except for Bart. BART So brilliant, so evil. MOE What drives a man to such villainy? (YELLING) Boo.

Rasputin tosses Krumnow out of the ring and into a group of SPECTATORS.

BART Now, those are great seats. Hey, Milhouse, turn it up.

Milhouse gets up off the couch to ADJUST the sound, and quickly Bart steals his seat.

MILHOUSE Hey, I was sitting there.

BART Right. Now I'm sitting here. Pass the Salt Doodles.

Milhouse hands Bart a box of weird twisty-shaped crackers covered entirely with salt.

Homer, sitting at the bar, is arguing with a Barney.

Homer gives Barney a guilty smile, which reveals bits of egg on his teeth. He BURPS.

EXT. SPRINGFIELD - MOMENTS LATER

The Simpson car driving down the road approaches camera. Homer, behind the wheel, races through Springfield with great determination. The car drives away from the camera and down the road.

Homer pulls into the driveway very fast and comes to a SCREECHING halt.

INT. SIMPSON LIVING ROOM - AT THAT MOMENT Bart hears the sound of Homer's car in the driveway. EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - CONTINUOUS Homer hurries out of his car. Bart runs out of the house and down the front path. Homer and Bart meet in the center of the path, finally face to face. They look into each others' eyes for a moment then blurt out the news. BART & HOMER TRUCKASAUROUS ! They hug. BART Jinx. You owe me a Coke, man. HOMER Why you little -- BART Two Cokes. INT. The orchestra PLAYS. We INTERCUT Homer, his watch, Marge, Lisa and Bart, building tension as the waltz plays out.

Largo gives Homer a look. After about a minute, the piece ends. Everyone is out of breath. The audience APPLAUDS.

As Largo and the STUDENTS take a bow, Homer runs up on stage and whisks Lisa away before she can finish her bows.

INT. SIMPSON CAR - NIGHT Homer is driving like a mad man through the streets of Springfield. He is passing cars on the shoulder, nearly hitting other cars, and being HONKED at all the while. LISA Faster, Dad. BART Truckasaurous awaits. MARGE Homer, be a defensive driver. HOMER Sometimes the best defense is a good offense. EXT. SPRINGFIELD SPEEDWAY - NIGHT Homer finally pulls into the parking lot of the Springfield Speedway. The marquee reads, "Tonight: Monster Truck Rally featuring Truckasaurous and Special Guest." And in smaller letters below: "Sunday Night: Bear Baiting". The dirt parking lot is filled to capacity with cars, trucks and vans which all have white trash bumper stickers on them: "Don't come a-knockin' if this van's a-rockin'"; and "Don't laugh, your daughter's in here" are a few

examples. Homer can't find a parking space anywhere and is getting mud all over his car from the dirt.

MARGE There's a space. Next aisle over. Homer makes a bee-line to the spot, but is cut off by a tough looking GUY in a truck. As the truck swerves, it covers Homer's windshield with mud. Homer drives on. Homer sees what appears to be open section in the speedway fence where he thinks he can find more parking. He drives through what looks like a service alley until he spins out in a pool of mud, which also coats the windshield. The car is stuck. The wheels of the car just spin as Homer tries to get the car moving. HOMER ] Damn unpaved backwoods general parking.

Lisa looks out the back window to see imposing metal FEET coming toward then.

LISA Dad?

HOMER Not now, Lisa.

Homer MUTTERS to himself as he tries to put the car into a gear that will run.

MARGE (WEAKLY) Homer. HOMER What? A GIANT SHADOW engulfs the car. BART Truckasaurous! HOMER (EXCITED) Where, where? (LOOKS UP;

SHRIEKS)

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 15.

EXT. SPEEDWAY - CONTINUOUS

We see that the Simpsons' car is being stalked by TRUCKASAUROUS. Homer gets the car to move about two feet, but then it gets caught in more mud. The crowd goes WILD as Truckasaurous moves closer and engulfs the frightened family in flames. Finally, the mammoth metal monster picks up the car in its gaping jaws and begins to crush the car, with the Simpsons in it. Above all the NOISE we hear the Simpsons SCREAM in terror.

FADE OUT.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 16.

FADE IN: EXT. SPRINGFIELD SPEEDWAY - NIGHT An EMERGENCY CREW is using blow torches and the jaws of life to extract Homer from the chewed body of the car. Marge, Lisa, Maggie, and Bart are off to the side watching the action. JACK SCRAPER and his TEAM of insurance adjusters, are walking around the scene of the accident. He is wearing a yellow plastic pancho and yellow hard hat that reads "AllStar Insurance Disaster Team." He walks up to Marge. MARGE We really appreciate the insurance company sending you out here like so quickly. SCRAPER That's what we're here for, Mrs. Simpson. He ‘looks down at his clip board. SCRAPER (CONT'D) Let's see, cracked windshield, melted bumpers, punctured radiator, teeth marks in the trunk and crushed transmission. Оп the plus side, there doesn't seem to be any frame damage. How did this thing happen? HOMER (FROM INSIDE CAR) It wasn't my fault

Truckasaurus jumped right out in front

of me.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 17.

SCRAPER Uh huh. Well, this check should take care off everything. Scraper writes out a check and hands it Marge. MARGE Thank you so much. He and his team walk away in formation. BART Mom, do we have to stay here and watch Dad get rescued? Can't we at least see the mud pull? MARGE Just hold your horses, Bart. At the car, rescue workers still try to free Homer. There is a large hole cut in the roof of the car and they try to pull Homer through the hole but he is too fat. RESCUE WORKER Mr. Simpson for the last time tell us your waist size. HOMER (FROM INSIDE THE CAR) I'm a size thirty-six. RESCUE WORKER Mr. Simpson if you were a thirty-six you'd of been out of here a long time ago.

Marge goes over to one of the rescue workers.

Homer has finished all the food in front of him, and he is picking his teeth with a ticket stub.

TRACK ANNOUNCER

For our last event of the evening we

have a special surprise guest. The

world's greatest Daredevil. The man who

laughs at danger. The man who tells

caution to take a hike: Captain Lance

Murdock. The lights dim and haunting bull fighter MUSIC blares over the PA system. A spotlight hits an empty part of the speedway. There is a massive EXPLOSION revealing CAPTAIN MURDOCK. He runs out from the flames into the open field

as the lights come up and people CHEER. It is clear that he is on fire.

CLOSE UP - BART Bart is captivated by the event. Still on fire, Murdock grabs a mic and begins to speak as several SPEEDWAY WORKERS, with foam shooting fire extinguishers, put the fire out. MURDOCK

Ladies, and gentlemen, and especially

little children, I'm glad you are all

here to witness what may very well be

my grisly death. I'm sure you remember

when I shattered my pelvis attempting

to leap over the Trevi Fountain in ;

Копе.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 20.

The crowd APPLAUDS appreciatively. MURDOCK (CONT'D) Others may remember my ill-fated attempt to launch myself into orbit and the six years I spent learning how to walk again. More APPLAUSE. MURDOCK (CONT'D) But tonight, my most dangerous stunt. I will defy both nature and gravity by leaping over this tank of water... A large steel water tank is revealed. MURDOCK (CONT'D)

.. filled with man eating great white sharks, deadly electric eels, starving piranhas, bone crushing alligators, and perhaps most frightening of all, the king of the jungle, one ferocious lion.

A large lion cage is lowered opened and tilted so that a LION falls into the tank. The crowd ООООНБ.

MURDOCK (CONT'D) And, just to make things interesting, I will add to this dangerous brew, one drop of human blood. Murdock walks up to the tank, pricks his finger with a needle and squeezes a drop of blood in the tank. The

SOUNDS of snapping jaws, electric current being discharged, bubbling water, and an occasional ROAR is heard.

0o — - 66—02...

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 21.

MURDOCK (CONT'D) On the chance that I don't survive, let me just say, buckle-up.

The Captain jumps on his tricked out motorcycle and SCREAMS off doing a wheelie. From an angle just above the water line inside the tank, we see shark fins circling, an eel slither by, and the lion ominously swims by with only it's eyes and nostrils above the water line.

BART '

This is so cool. The Captain rides back once, doing a wheelie and inspecting the ramp. There is an OMINOUS DRUM ROLL. The crowd is hushed. The motorcycle REVS and Murdock takes off toward the ramp.

HOMER I can't watch.

Homer covers his eyes. The camera PULLS BACK to see Marge and Lisa put their hands over their eyes. Marge takes one hand and puts it over Maggie's eyes.

PULL BACK again to see the entire crowd has their eyes covered. Bart is the only one with his eyes open, and he is in heaven. Just as Murdock's cycle hits the ramp, it flips over. Murdock is flung at high speed up the ramp with the force of the crash. He tries to slow his fall by flinging his arms out and grabbing something, but he continues to roll end over end toward the tank of doom. Тһе audience GASPS as he falls into the tank where he is savagely attacked, bitten and shocked by the animals. Through the furiously bubbling water one can only see the flurry of activity and occasional electric flashes. Murdock valiantly fights his way toward the side of the tank and just about pulls himself out when, from out of nowhere, the lion violently grabs Murdock and drags him back in the tank. The Simpsons watch on in horror, all except Bart who sees the beauty in the stunt.

BART

Bitchin'!

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 22.

We see Murdock being dragged out of the tank by RESCUE WORKERS. Murdock is badly hurt, bitten, and bloody. He still attempts to swagger as the rescue team straps him into the gurney. Murdock gives the crowd a very shaky thumbs up just before he blacks out. TRACK ANNOUNCER He's okay, folks. The crowd CHEERS. TRACK ANNOUNCER (CONT'D) That concludes the entertainment

portion of the evening.

The bright white flood lights are turned on throughout the stadium, signaling the end of the evening.

INT. SIMPSONS CAR - NIGHT Homer and the family are somehow driving slowly home in the badly damaged car. Marge's hair sticks out of the hole in the roof, blowing violently in the wind. Cars HONK and pass The Simpsons as they fondly remember their evening. MARGE What a fun-filled evening. What was everybody's favorite part? HOMER That's easy, the nachos. LISA

What was your favorite part, Bart?

Bart doesn't answer because he's lost in a daydream. The camera enters Bart's eye and we see:

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 23.

Bart imagines himself, sightly better built, wearing the exact same outfit as Captain Murdock, and riding around on a skateboard. Bart gives a thumbs up at the end of the ramp and, as the crowd ROARS, races up toward the tank. Bart flies up through the air and drops directly into the tank, where he is bitten ferociously and pulled from the tank by rescue workers. Bart, mauled and bleeding, is put on a stretcher. Bart gathers his strength to wave to the crowd. The crowd CHEERS and gets on their feet CHANTING "Bart, Bart, Bart...." The crowd noise fades in the distance as Marge's voice is heard more clearly saying: LISA (0.S.) (CONT'D) Bart, Bart, Bart...

As the camera slowly leaves Bart's eye, there is a new expression of determination on Bart's face.

LISA (CONT'D) Bart, what was your favorite part? BART When I found what I was put on this Earth for. HOMER Yeah, those were some nachos. LONG SHOT The nearly destroyed car slowly rolls down the street. EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - DAY Bart is setting up a ramp as Lewis, Milhouse and Richard, stand around in the Simpson driveway. Bart drags the ramp over so that it is just above the grill of Marge's car. He is obviously preparing to jump the car length-wise. MILHOUSE You're crazy, Bart.

RICHARD

You're never gonna make it.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 24.

BART Spare me your lectures, ladies. Bart puts on a football helmet, steps on his skateboard and takes off. He heads toward the ramp with as much speed as he can muster. Bart hits the ramp and the skateboard wobbles off in one direction, Bart flying off the in the other. His head bounces off the windshield. BART (CONT'D) Oof! He winds up in a heap on the driveway. LEWIS Oh no! He's hurt! RICHARD Bad! MILHOUSE Let's get out of here! They scatter. EXT. SPRINGFIELD HOSPITAL - DAY The large white building with a large sign saying “Springfield General Hospital" There is a smaller sign underneath it reading, "Cash Only." INT. EMERGENCY WAITING AREA - DAY A worried Marge sits with Lisa in the sparsely furnished waiting area along with other people, some of whom are waiting for medical attention. All eyes turn as DOCTOR JULIUS HIBBERT, a black man, every bit a professional, enters the room with Bart. Bart has a small bandage where he hurt himself. Marge runs up to Bart and hugs him. MARGE Oh, my little boy, are you all right? BART Better than all right, I got stitches.

Bart lifts his patch up so Lisa can peek inside.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 25.

LISA Ewwww. BART (PROUDLY) Probably have a scar too. HIBBERT Mrs. Simpson, Bart tells me he injured himself training for a career in death defiance. MARGE Yes, well, we saw a daredevil last night and well, you know, monkey see, monkey do. HIBBERT Hmmm. I think I know something that might discourage him from this sort of behavior. INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - DAY Doctor Hibbert walks with Bart as Marge and Lisa follow. HIBBERT Bart, in this ward are the children who have been hurt by imitating stunts they Saw on television, movies and the legitmate stage.

She stops in front of two opaque doors. She swings open the doors.

INT. HOSPITAL WARD - DAY

The room is filled with KIDS in various stages of 111 i health. Each bed features a child with a different injury.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 26.

НІВВЕКТ This little boy broke both his legs when he tried to fly like Superman.

They walk to the next bed.

HIBBERT (CONT'D) This young girl suffered two scratched corneas when her brother poked her in the eyes after watching the Three Stooges.

They walk on passing a BOY with his head wrapped in bandages.

HIBBERT (CONT'D) And this is her brother who, in the same regrettable incident, was hit in the head with a shovel.

MARGE Gee, I never realized TV was such a dangerous influence.

DR. HIBBERT

(TO MARGE) Well, actually, it's a two- edged sword. Watching years of Marcus Welby M.D inspired me to become a doctor. (TO BART) Bart, I hope I made my point.

BART You certainly did, Doctor.

They all exit. Bart sticks his head back in the door.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 27.

BART (TO KIDS) So long, dudes. Save me a bed. EXT. SPRINGFIELD STREET - DAY

A Springfield Unified School District bus rolls down the highway filled with raucous KIDS.

INT. BUS - DAY 4 Kids throw wads of paper, SCREAM, CRY, and generally FROLIC as the bus driven by OTTO rolls down the highway. MRS. KRABAPPEL sits in the front seat thumbing through a magazine. Bart stares out the window, concentrating on the view. Milhouse, sitting in the next seat, watches Bart. MILHOUSE Bart, I hope you learned your lesson. BART Milhouse, the greatest thrill of my life was banging off that windshield. I'm searching for my next big stunt. It's out there somewhere. I can feel it in my bones. ZOOM IN on Bart. We are in Bart's world and can no longer hear the kids in the background. Bart stares out the window as the bus passes twenty cars tightly packed together. BART (У.О.) (CON'T) Those cars give me an idea, but it's been done. The bus then passes a gully. BART (V.0.) (CONT'D)

Gully? Naw, too survivable.

The bus passes a waterfall.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 28.

BART (У.О.) (CONT'D) Too old fashioned. The SOUND of the bus squeaking to a stop bleeds into Bart's revery. Bart can hear what is going on as he files out of the bus behind all the other kids. EXT. SPRINGFIELD GORGE - CONTINUOUS Bart steps off the bus, and there standing in front of a giant picturesque gorge is RANGER SMYTHE, a very burly and outdoorsy female forest ranger, who sweeps her arm indicating the natural wonder. The gorge is deep, wide, and rocky with a raging river that runs through the center. SMYTHE Hello children, and welcome to Springfield Gorge. CLOSE UP - BART

His eyes are wide with excitement as he walks closer to the edge. Bart has found the sight of his next stunt.

BART Destiny, at last we meet.

We hear an ECHO of Bart's words, "we meet, we meet, we meet."

FADE OUT.

Otto walks with Bart. SMYTHE l Teddy Roosevelt once called Springfield Gorge the third most breathtaking gorge in this part of the state. Otto and Bart, taking in the gorge, lag behind the group. OTTO (IMPRESSED) Man, this thing's pretty gnarly. I bet you could throw a dead body in there and no one'd ever find it. Ranger Smythe continues her lecture to the kids. SMYTHE ...And when trail-blazing Jebediah Springfield came upon this glorious chasm, legend has it he spoke his famous words, "We'll never make it across that thing." And, thus, the town of Springfield was born.

Bart raises his hand.

BART Excuse me, ma'am, how wide is this gorge? SMYTHE Sixty-three feet. BART How much is that in busses? SMITH Seven. MRS. KRABAPPEL Well, Bart Simpson, it's a pleasure to see you finally take an interest in academics. BART Thank you, ma'am. SMYTHE Now, if you'll follow me, I'll show you the historic sight of Springfield's first snack bar and souvenir shop. The Ranger leads the class away. BART Otto, I'm gonna leap over Springfield Gorge on my skateboard. OTTO You know, Bart, as the only adult here

I feel I should say something.

LISA Hey, Mom, who took all the stars off of my exam papers? MARGE I don't know. Sometimes your brother takes things that don't belong to hin. INT. BART'S ROOM - CONTINUOUS Bart is posing in front of a mirror. Spelled out in shining stars on his t-shirt is "Born 2 Leap". He also wears Homer's underwear as a cape. Lisa enters. LISA Bart, what are you doing? He turns to her. BART Puttin' on my work clothes, Lis. Pretty dazzling, huh? How do you think I'll look hurtling over Springfield Gorge in these? LISA Springfield Gorge? Bart, you could get

killed.

BART Either way, it's a show. LISA Please, before you do anything, there's someone I'd like you to talk to. EXT. SPRINGFIELD HOSPITAL HALLWAY - DAY

LISA А Thank you for arranging this, Dr. Hibbert. DR. HIBBERT Anything I can do to stem the tide of entertainment-related injury. Dr. Hibbert pulls open a hospital curtain revealing LANCE MURDOCK, who is completely immobile. His arms and legs are in traction and he sports a full body cast. Bart is unnerved. DR. HIBBERT (CONT'D) Lance, this is Lisa and Bart Simpson. Bart is one of your biggest fans. Lance puts on a brave face for Bart. BART It's an honor, Lance. How are you feeling? Lance HUFFS and PUFFS, sweats and strains, as he struggles to lift his thumb to the "thumbs-up" position. He finally accomplishes this task in obvious agony. A loud CRACK is heard.

MURDOCK

Doc. І heard а пар.

(TO LISA) Squealer.

HOMER

Bart, we forbid you to jump over that gorge. BART You can't. HOMER We can and do. Go to your room, Bart!

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 37.

BART Hey, man, you can tell me not to do it, but there's no way you can watch me twenty-four hours a day. And the minute your back is turned, I'm grabbing my skateboard and heading for the gorge. Bart storms off to his room. HOMER He's got us, Marge. There's nothing we can do. He's as good as dead. Homer puts his head in his hands and begins to SOB. BART'S ROOM - LATER

Homer sits on the bed next to Bart. He is demonstrating the folly of Bart's jump using a toy motorcycle.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 38.

HOMER Bart, say this motorcycle is you and the dresser is the other side of the gorge. I'm no scientist, but look what's going to happen. Homer revs up the toy motorcycle and lets it go. It sails off the bed and lands on the dresser, executing a perfect landing and coming to a smart 90 degree stop. HOMER (CONT'D) Okay, bad example. Marge appears in the doorway. HOMER (CONT'D) Listen, son, there are lots of things I like about being a father... (SITS А MOMENT, STUMPED) I think you get a tax deduction. But one of the many things I don't like is punishing you. If you just promise me you won't jump over that gorge, I'll be happy to let you out of this room and play in the yard. BART Okay, I promise.

Bart starts to leave his room, but Marge stops hin.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 39.

MARGE Now, just wait a second. Son, do you know what a promise is? It's a sacred oath. I know it may seem cool to risk your life, but nothing is cooler than a young man who keeps his word to his parents. Do you understand what I'm saying? BART Yes, ma'am. HOMER So with that in mind, promise us that you won't try to jump over that gorge. BART (SOLEMNLY) I promise. HOMER That's my boy. Marge smiles. Homer and Marge exit. Bart starts to WHISTLE, grabs his costume and skateboard and heads out the window. EXT. SPRINGFIELD GORGE - DAY A large GROUP OF KIDS have turned out to see Bart jump over the gorge. Bart arrives wearing his "Born 2 Leap" uniform and carrying his skateboard. Bart eyes the ramp for a few moments, puts his skateboard in the ready position, and prepares to go. Just then he stops himself, and tests the wind with his finger. He gives the thumbs up and prepares to go when the Simpsons SCREECH up in their car. Homer runs out, followed by the whole family. HOMER

Don't do it, boy!

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 40.

BART I've got to, Dad. I know I can do it. HOMER But you promised. BART I had my fingers crossed. HOMER Damn. Homer slowly approaches Bart on the ramp. HOMER (CONT'D) Son, you can try it. I won't try and stop you, but if you jump, then I want to jump across this Springfield Gorge, too. BART But... Homer walks up the ramp. The rest of the family stands below, filled with fear. Homer takes the skateboard, and nervously puts his foot on it. HOMER (NERVOUS) No, Bart, I won't let you do it unless it's safe, so I'll go first. BART No, Dad, you'll never make it. It's too windy, and the rocks are slippery, and sixty-three feet is a lot farther

than I thought it was.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 41.

HOMER Sounds like you're having second thoughts. BART Are you kidding? I'm terrified. The only thing that scares me more than certain death is all my friends calling - me chicken. HOMER Don't worry, son. I'll tell them I made you do it. BART Thanks, Dad. Let's get outta here. HOMER Oh, thank God. Homer still has one foot on the skateboard. His other foot lifts off the ground and he begins to suddenly travel at great speed down the ramp. Homer SCREAMS as he hits the lip of the gorge and is hurled into empty space. We see Homer's POV of the far gorge wall getting slightly closer and Homer's view of the long way down into the gorge. Homer continues to speed toward the other edge of the gorge. It looks as if he actually might make it. HOMER (CONT'D) (EXCITED) I'm going to make it! I'm going to make it! Bart was right, this is the greatest thrill of my life! I'm king of the world! Homer begins to descend and doesn't come close to making it, smashing into the far gorge wall. He tumbles down the rock face, smashing into little ledges along the way, until !

he lands at the bottom іп a pile of mud. Не tries to raise his head and is hit on the head by the falling skateboard.

TABLE DRAFT 5/4/90 PAGE 42.

An ambulance pulls up. Two MEN grab Homer and put him in the ambulance. The ambulance speeds away, drives ten feet and smashes into a tree. INT. SPRINGFIELD HOSPITAL - DAY. Homer is lying in traction. BART Sorry, Homer. HOMER Son, even though I'm in agony, it's worth it if you learned a lesson. Still want to jump the gorge? BART No way. I'd have to be an idiot to try something like that. PULL BACK to reveal Lance Murdock in the next bed. HOMER (TO LANCE) You think you've got guts, try raising my kids. FADE OUT.

ІНЕ END
